author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us praise the Lord both in voice, and in understanding, and in good works; and, as this Psalm exhorteth, let us sing unto Him a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It beginneth: “Sing ye to the Lord a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His praise is in the Church of the Saints” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The old man hath an old song, the new man a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Old Testament is an old song, the New Testament a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the Old Testament are temporal and earthly promises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso loveth earthly things singeth an old song: let him that desireth to sing a new song, love the things of eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love itself is new and eternal; therefore is it ever new, because it never groweth old.…And this song is of peace, this song is of charity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso severeth himself from the union of the saints, singeth not a new song; for he hath followed old strife, not new charity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In new charity what is there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peace, the bond of an holy society, a spiritual union, a building of living stones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in one place, but throughout the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is said in another Psalm, “Sing unto the Lord, all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From this is understood, that he who singeth not with the whole earth, singeth an old song, whatever words proceed out of his mouth.…We have already said, brethren, that all the earth singeth a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who singeth not with the whole earth a new song, let him sing what he will, let his tongue sound forth Halleluia, let him utter it all day and all night, my ears are not so much bent to hear the voice of the singer, but I seek the deeds of the doer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I ask, and say, “What is it that thou singest?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He answereth, “Halleluia.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “Halleluia”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise ye the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Come, let us praise the Lord together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou praisest the Lord, and I praise the Lord, why are we at variance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Charity praiseth the Lord, discord blasphemeth the Lord.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The field of the Lord is the world, not Africa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not with the Lord’s field, as it is without these fields of ours, where Getulia bears sixty or an hundred fold, Numidia only ten fold: everywhere fruit is borne to Him, both an hundred fold, and sixty fold, and thirty fold: only do thou choose what thou wilt be, if thou thinkest to belong to the Lord’s Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Church” then “of the saints” is the Catholic Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church of the saints is not the Church of heretics.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church of the saints is that which God first prefigured before it was seen, and then set forth that it might be seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church of the saints was heretofore in writings, now it is in nations: the Church of the saints was heretofore only read of, now it is both read of and seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When it was only read of, it was believed; now it is seen, and is spoken against.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His praise is in the “children of the kingdom,” that is, “the Church of the saints.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Israel rejoice in Him who made Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Israel”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Seeing God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who seeth God, rejoiceth in Him by whom he was made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it then, brethren? we have said that we belong to the Church of the saints: do we already see God? and how are we Israel, if we see not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is one kind of sight belonging to this present time; there will be another belonging to the time hereafter: the sight which now is, is by faith; the sight which is to be will be in reality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we believe, we see; if we love, we see: see what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ask John: “God is love;” let us bless His holy Name, and rejoice in God by rejoicing in love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso hath love, why send we him afar to see God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him regard his own conscience, and there he seeth God.…“And let the sons of Sion exult in their King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sons of the Church are Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Sion indeed was one city, which fell: amid its ruins certain saints dwelt after the flesh: but the true Sion, the true Jerusalem (for Sion and Jerusalem are one), is “eternal in the heavens,” and is “our mother.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She it is that hath given us birth, she is the Church of the saints, she hath nourished us, she, who is in part a pilgrim, in part abiding in the heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the part which abideth in heaven is the bliss of angels, in the part which wandereth in this world is the hope of the righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the former is said, “Glory to God in the highest;” of the latter, “and on earth peace to men of good will.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let those then who, being in this life, groan, and long for their country, run by love, not by bodily feet; let them seek not ships but wings, let them lay hold on the two wings of love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the two wings of love?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The love of God, and of our neighbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now we are pilgrims, we sigh, we groan.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There has come to us a letter from our country: we read it to you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the sons of Sion shall exult in their King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Son of God, who made us, was made one of us: and He rules us as our King, because He is our Creator, who made us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He by whom we were made is the same as He by whom we are ruled, and we are Christians because He is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is called Christ from Chrism, that is, Anointing.…Give to the Priest somewhat to offer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What could man find which he could give as a clean victim?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What victim? what clean thing can a sinner offer?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O unrighteous, O sinful man, whatever thou offerest is unclean, and somewhat that is clean must be offered for thee.…Let then the Priest that is clean offer Himself, and cleanse thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is what Christ did.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He found in man nothing clean for Him to offer for man: He offered Himself as a clean Victim.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Happy Victim, true Victim, spotless Offering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He offered not then what we gave Him; yea rather, He offered what He took of us, and offered it clean.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of us He took flesh, and this He offered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But where took He it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the womb of the Virgin Mary, that He might offer it clean for us unclean.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is our King, He is our Priest, in Him let us rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them praise His Name in chorus” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth “chorus”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many know what a “chorus” is: nay, as we are speaking in a town, almost all know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A “chorus” is the union of singers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we sing “in chorus,” let us sing in concord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If any one’s voice is out of harmony in a chorus of singers, it offendeth the ear, and throweth the chorus into confusion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the voice of one echoing discordantly troubleth the harmony of them who sing, how doth the discord of heresy throw into confusion the harmony of them who praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole world is now the chorus of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The chorus of Christ soundeth harmoniously from east to west.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them sing a psalm unto Him with timbrel and psaltery.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore taketh he to him the “timbrel and psaltery”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That not the voice alone may praise, but the works too.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When timbrel and psaltery are taken, the hands harmonize with the voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So too do thou, whensoever thou singest “Halleluia,” deal forth thy bread to the hungry, clothe the naked, take in the stranger: then doth not only thy voice sound, but thy hand soundeth in harmony with it, for thy deeds agree with thy words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast taken to thee an instrument, and thy fingers agree with thy tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor must we keep back the mystical meaning of the “timbrel and psaltery.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the timbrel leather is stretched, on the psaltery gut is stretched; on either instrument the flesh is crucified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How well did he “sing a psalm on timbrel and psaltery,” who said, “the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This psaltery or timbrel He wishes thee to take up, who loveth a new song, who teacheth thee, saying to thee, “Whosoever willeth to be My disciple, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him not set down his psaltery, let him not set down his timbrel, let him stretch himself out on the wood, and be dried from the lust of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The more the strings are stretched, the more sharply do they sound.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul then, in order that his psaltery might sound sharply, what said he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Stretching forth unto those things which are before,” etc. He stretched himself: Christ touched him; and the sweetness of truth sounded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord hath dealt kindly among His people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dealing so kindly, as to die for the ungodly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dealing so kindly, as with righteous Blood to blot out the handwriting against the sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dealing so kindly, as to say, “I regard not what ye were, be ye now what ye were not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He dealeth kindly in converting him that was turned away, in aiding him that is fighting, in crowning the conqueror.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the meek He shall lift up in salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the proud too are lifted up, but not in salvation: the meek are lifted in salvation, the proud in death: that is, the proud lift up themselves, and God humbleth them: the meek humble themselves, and God lifteth them up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The saints shall exult in glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would say somewhat important about the glory of the saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is no one who loveth not glory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the glory of fools, popular glory as it is called, hath snares to deceive, so that a man, influenced by the praises of vain men, shall be willing to live in such fashion as to be spoken of by men, whosoever they be, in whatsoever way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence it is that men, rendered mad, and puffed up with pride, empty within, without swollen, are willing ever to ruin their fortunes by bestowing them on stage-players, actors, men who fight with wild beasts, charioteers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sums they give, what sums they spend!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They lavish the powers not only of their patrimony, but of their minds too.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They scorn the poor, because the people shouteth not that the poor should be given to, but the people do shout that the fighter with wild beasts be given to.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then no shout is raised to them, they refuse to spend; when madmen shout to them, they are mad too: nay, all are mad, both performer, and spectator, and the giver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This mad glory is blamed by the Lord, is offensive in the eyes of the Almighty.…Thou choosest to clothe the fighter with wild beasts, who may be beaten, and make thee blush: Christ is never conquered; He hath conquered the devil, He hath conquered for thee, and to thee, and in thee; such a conqueror as this thou choosest not to clothe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because there is less shouting, less madness about it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then who delight in such glory, have an empty conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as they drain their chests, to send garments as presents, so do they empty their conscience, so as to have nothing precious therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the saints who “exult in glory,” no need is there for us to say how they exult: just hear the verse of the Psalm which followeth: “The saints shall exult in glory, they shall rejoice in their beds:” not in theatres, or amphitheatres, or circuses, or follies, or market places, but “in their chambers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in their chambers”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In their hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle Paul exulting in his closet: “For this is our glory, the testimony of our conscience.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the other hand, there is reason to fear lest any be pleasing to himself, and so seem to be proud, and boast of his conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For every one ought to exult with fear, for that wherein he exulteth is God’s gift, not his own desert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there be many that please themselves, and think themselves righteous; and there is another passage which goeth against them, which saith, “Who shall boast that he hath a clean heart, and that he is pure from sin?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is then, so to speak, a limit to glorying in our conscience, namely, to know that thy faith is sincere, thy hope sure, thy love without dissimulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The exultations of God are in their mouths” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such wise shall they “rejoice in their closets,” as not to attribute to themselves that they are good, but praise Him from whom they have what they are, by whom they are called to attain to what they are not, and from whom they hope for perfection, to whom they give thanks, because He hath begun.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And swords sharpened on both sides in their hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This sort of weapon contains a great mystical meaning, in that it is sharp on both sides.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By “swords sharpened on both sides,” we understand the Word of the Lord: it is one sword, but therefore are they called many, because there are many mouths and many tongues of the saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is it two edged?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It speaks of things temporal, it speaks also of things eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In both cases it proveth what it saith, and him whom it strikes, it severeth from the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not this the sword whereof the Lord said, “I am not come to send peace upon earth, but a sword”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how He came to divide, how He came to sever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He divideth the saints, He divideth the ungodly, He severeth from thee that which hindereth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The son willeth to serve God, the father willeth not: the sword cometh, the Word of God cometh, and severeth the son from the father.…Wherefore then is it in their hands, not in their tongues?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And swords,” it saith, “sharpened on both sides in their hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By “in their hands,” he meaneth in power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They received then the word of God in power, to speak where they would, to whom they would, neither to fear power, nor to despise poverty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they had in their hands a sword; where they would they brandished it, handled it, smote with it: and all this was in the power of the preachers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if the Word be not in their hands, why is it written, “The Word of the Lord was put in the hand of the Prophet Haggai”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely, brethren, God set not His Word in His fingers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by, “was put in his hand”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was put into his power to preach the Word of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, we can understand these “hands” in another way also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they who spake had the word of God in their tongues, they who wrote, in their hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, brethren, ye see the saints armed: observe the slaughter, observe their glorious battles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there be a commander, there must be soldiers; if soldiers, an enemy; if a warfare, a victory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What have these done who had in their hands swords sharpened on both sides?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To do vengeance on the nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See whether vengeance have not been done on the nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Daily is it done: we do it ourselves by speaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how the nations of Babylon are slain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She is repaid twofold: for so is it written of her, “repay her double for what she hath done.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is she repaid double?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The saints wage war, they draw their “swords twice sharpened;” thence come defeats, slaughters, severances: how is she repaid double?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When she had power to persecute the Christians, she slew the flesh indeed, but she crushed not God: now she is repaid double, for the Pagans are extinguished and the idols are broken.…And lest thou shouldest think that men are really smitten with the sword, blood really shed, wounds made in the flesh, he goeth on and explaineth, “upbraidings among the peoples.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “upbraidings”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the “sword twice sharpened” go forth from you, delay not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say to thy friend, if yet thou hast one left to whom to say it, “What kind of man art thou, who hast abandoned Him by whom thou wast made, and worshippest what He made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Better is the Workman, than that which He worketh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When he beginneth to blush, when he beginneth to feel compunction, thou hast made a wound with thy sword, it hath reached the heart, he is about to die, that he may live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That they may bind their kings in fetters, and their nobles in bonds of iron” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To execute upon them the judgment written” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The kings of the Gentiles are to be bound in fetters, “and their nobles in fetters,” and that “of iron.”…For these verses which we are beginning to explain are obscure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For for this purpose God willed to set down some of His verses obscurely, not that anything new should be dug out of them, but that what was already well known, might be made new by being obscurely set forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know that kings have been made Christians; we know that the nobles of the Gentiles have been made Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are being made so at this day; they have been, they shall be; the “swords twice sharpened” are not idle in the hands of the saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then do we understand their being bound in fetters and chains of iron?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye know, beloved and learned brethren (learned I call you, for ye have been nourished in the Church, and are accustomed to hear God’s Word read), that “God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the strong, and the foolish things of the world hath God chosen to confound the wise, and things which are not, just as things which are, that the things which are may be brought to nought.”…It is said by the Lord, “If thou wilt be perfect, go sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor, and come, follow Me, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many of the nobles did this, but they ceased to be nobles of the Gentiles, they chose rather to be poor in this world, noble in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But many retain their former nobility, retain their royal powers, and yet are Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are, as it were, “in fetters and in bonds of iron.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How so? they received fetters, to keep them from going to things unlawful, the “fetters of wisdom,” the fetters of the Word of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then are they bonds of iron and not bonds of gold?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are iron so long as they fear: let them love, and they shall be golden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe, beloved, what I say.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard just now the Apostle John, “There is no fear in love, but perfect love casteth out fear, because fear hath torment.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the bond of iron.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet unless a man begin through fear to worship God, he will not attain to love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The beginning then is bonds of iron, the end a collar of gold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said of wisdom, “a collar of gold around thy neck.”…There cometh to us a man powerful in this world, his wife offendeth him, and perhaps he hath desired another man’s wife who is more beautiful, or another woman who is richer, he wisheth to put away the one he hath, yet he doeth it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He heareth the words of the servant of God, he heareth the Prophet, he heareth the Apostle, and he doeth it not; he is told by one in whose hands is a “sword twice sharpened,” “Thou shalt not do it: it is not lawful for thee: God alloweth thee not to put away thy wife, “save for the cause of fornication.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He heareth this, he feareth, and doeth it not.…Listen, young men; the bonds are of iron, seek not to set your feet within them; if ye do, ye shall be bound more tightly with fetters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such fetters the hands of the Bishop make strong for you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not men who are thus fettered fly to the Church, and are here loosed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men do fly hither, desiring to be rid of their wives: here they are more tightly bound: no man looseth these fetters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What God joined together, let not man put asunder.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these bonds are hard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who but knows it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hardness the Apostles grieved at, and said, “If this be the case with a wife, it is not good to marry.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the bonds be of iron, it is not good to set our feet within them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Lord said, “All men cannot receive this saying, but let him that can receive it, receive it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Art thou bound unto a wife? seek not to be freed,” for thou art bound with bonds of iron.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Art thou free from a wife, seek not a wife;” bind not thyself with bonds of iron.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To do in them the judgment that is written.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the judgment which the saints do throughout all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore “written”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because these things were before written, and now are fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold now they are being done: erst they were read, and were not done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he hath concluded thus, “this glory have all His saints.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Throughout the whole world, throughout entire nations, this the saints do, thus are they glorified, thus do they “exalt God with their mouths,” thus do they “rejoice in their beds,” thus do they “exult in their glory,” thus are they “lifted up in salvation,” thus do they “sing a new song,” thus in heart and voice and life they say Halleluia.
